limitations.label: Settings evidence boundary
limitations.detail: LabKit can verify local settings and schema/docs coverage. It cannot prove account entitlement, provider IAM, managed policy precedence, or live Claude Code runtime behavior without an active Claude Code session.

control.notes: Local settings can be confirmed from disk; use a fresh runtime session for live proof.

verification_steps.label: Settings file
verification_steps.detail: Confirm the selected settings scope contains the value LabKit writes.

dependencies.label: Settings precedence
dependencies.detail: Claude Code applies managed policy, CLI arguments, local, project, and user settings with higher-precedence layers able to override lower ones.

dependencies.label: Fresh Claude Code session
dependencies.detail: Start a new Claude Code session after changing settings so the runtime reloads them.

setting.notes: Local settings can be confirmed from disk; use a fresh runtime session for live proof.

verification_steps.label: Claude Code status
verification_steps.detail: For live proof, inspect the relevant Claude Code slash command such as `/status`, `/config`, `/hooks`, `/mcp`, `/memory`, `/permissions`, `/model`, or `/context`.

dependencies.label: Session environment
dependencies.detail: Claude Code reads `settings.json` `env` values for Claude Code sessions. MCP child processes need per-server `env` configuration instead of relying on session env inheritance.

env-setting.notes: Local settings can be confirmed from disk; use a fresh runtime session for live proof.

verification_steps.label: Effective environment
verification_steps.detail: Use `/status`, provider-specific diagnostics, or a harmless runtime smoke test to prove the environment switch affected the current session.

dependencies.label: Schema-discovered setting
dependencies.detail: This key is present in the fetched or bundled SchemaStore JSON Schema.

limitations.label: Schema can lag
limitations.detail: Anthropic documents that the published schema is updated periodically and may lag the newest Claude Code settings.

limitations.label: Schema shape, not semantics
limitations.detail: Schema presence proves key shape, not that your installed Claude Code build or account honors the behavior.

schema-setting.notes: Schema presence proves shape, not that your installed build honors the behavior.

dependencies.label: Existing settings key
dependencies.detail: This key was found in the selected settings file but is not curated by LabKit.

limitations.label: Observed only
limitations.detail: LabKit can preserve and display this setting, but it does not claim docs or schema confirmation.

settings-file.notes: This key was observed locally but is not curated by LabKit.

dependencies.label: Explicit session launch
dependencies.detail: This control is activated by a Claude Code command-line flag or launch mode, not by editing settings.json.

limitations.label: Reference-only
limitations.detail: LabKit intentionally does not enable or disable session-only controls.

manual.notes: Reference-only control; LabKit documents it but does not write settings for it.

verification_steps.label: Launch proof
verification_steps.detail: Start Claude Code with the documented session flag and confirm the active mode in-session.

limitations.label: Model and plan dependent
limitations.detail: Context, thinking, and prompt-cache behavior can depend on model family, account plan, provider route, and current Claude Code version.

limitations.label: Hook security
limitations.detail: Command hooks run with the user's full permissions. Async hooks cannot block tool calls or return decisions after the triggering action has already continued.

Automation.notes: Automation can run local commands or background work; inspect configured handlers before enabling.

limitations.label: Permission limits
limitations.detail: Read/Edit permission rules do not stop arbitrary subprocesses from reading or writing files. Use sandboxing or OS policy for shell-level isolation.

Safety.notes: Safety-related settings should be verified in a fresh session before relying on them.

dependencies.label: Network and provider access
dependencies.detail: Claude Code requires network access for model calls, and provider routes require their provider credentials and quotas.

Connectivity.notes: Provider and network controls can change routing, credentials, and traffic behavior.

limitations.label: External tool behavior
limitations.detail: MCP servers, plugins, browsers, and shell tools may have their own auth, installation, timeout, or policy gates outside LabKit's control.

limitations.label: Parallelism costs real quota
limitations.detail: Agent teams, background sessions, and forked subagents can multiply token usage, local processes, and file-conflict risk.

Agent Runtime.notes: Agent runtime features can multiply work, quota use, and file-conflict risk.

dependencies.label: Supported IDE integration
dependencies.detail: IDE controls require the matching Claude Code IDE extension/plugin and supported IDE version.

limitations.label: Network observation needed
limitations.detail: Local settings can request telemetry or traffic behavior, but proving no packets were sent requires network-level observation.

Privacy & Diagnostics.notes: Privacy and diagnostic settings need runtime or network observation for strong proof.

limitations.label: Not persistent
limitations.detail: These modes are chosen when launching Claude Code and are not durable settings.

Session Flags.notes: Command-only/session-only controls are visible but not written by LabKit.

limitations.label: Command-only
limitations.detail: These controls document runtime commands. LabKit does not execute them because they are interactive or account-billed workflows.

Session Commands.notes: Command-only/session-only controls are visible but not written by LabKit.

sources.label: AY Automate Claude Code leak analysis

limitations.label: Reference-only leak intelligence
limitations.detail: These entries come from public leak analysis rather than supported Anthropic docs. LabKit intentionally will not write unofficial, internal, or unsafe toggles.

limitations.label: May be build-gated or server-gated
limitations.detail: A string found in a leaked source map does not prove that a public Claude Code build contains, exposes, or honors the behavior.

Leaked / Internal.notes: Public leak-analysis only. LabKit shows these entries for transparency and will not enable unsupported or unsafe toggles.

verification_steps.label: Official availability check
verification_steps.detail: Use current official docs, `claude --version`, `/help`, `/status`, and the command list in a fresh Claude Code session before assuming any leaked feature works.

dependencies.label: 1M context eligibility
dependencies.detail: The active plan, model, and provider route must support 1M context. The setting can only keep the feature visible or opt out.

limitations.label: Cannot prove offline
limitations.detail: LabKit cannot prove 1M context eligibility from settings alone; use `/context` or runtime session evidence inside Claude Code.

verification_steps.label: Context command
verification_steps.detail: Start a fresh session and inspect `/context` or active model/context output.

1m-context.notes: Keeps 1M context visible when your account, model, provider route, and build support it; LabKit cannot prove entitlement offline.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.59+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe auto memory as requiring Claude Code v2.1.59 or newer.

limitations.label: Local memory only
limitations.detail: Auto memory is machine-local and memory-file loading has size/line limits documented by Anthropic.

verification_steps.label: Memory view
verification_steps.detail: Use `/memory` in Claude Code to inspect active memory sources.

auto-memory.notes: Memory is local and useful, but inspect active memory sources when privacy matters.

verification_steps.label: Hooks view
verification_steps.detail: Use `/hooks` to inspect configured hook events, matchers, source files, and handlers.

hooks.notes: Hooks can execute local commands with your permissions; review handlers before trusting them.

limitations.label: Provider exceptions
limitations.detail: Official docs note MCP tool search is enabled by default with exceptions for Vertex and non-first-party ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL routes.

tool-search.notes: Usually safe; provider routes can still gate availability.

dependencies.label: Amazon Bedrock credentials
dependencies.detail: Requires CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK, AWS_REGION, AWS credentials or AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, and Bedrock model access.

bedrock.notes: Routes traffic and credentials through Amazon Bedrock; verify provider IAM and region configuration.

dependencies.label: Google Vertex credentials
dependencies.detail: Requires CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX, CLOUD_ML_REGION, GCP authentication, and a project through ANTHROPIC_VERTEX_PROJECT_ID or GCP defaults.

vertex.notes: Routes traffic and credentials through Google Vertex AI; verify project and region configuration.

dependencies.label: Microsoft Foundry credentials
dependencies.detail: Requires CLAUDE_CODE_USE_FOUNDRY, Azure auth or ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_API_KEY, and ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_RESOURCE or ANTHROPIC_FOUNDRY_BASE_URL.

foundry.notes: Routes traffic and credentials through Microsoft Foundry; verify Azure auth and resource configuration.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.139+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe Agent View as a research preview requiring Claude Code v2.1.139 or later.

limitations.label: Local supervisor
limitations.detail: Background sessions run on your machine and stop when the machine sleeps or shuts down; each active session consumes normal account quota.

verification_steps.label: Agent view
verification_steps.detail: Run `claude agents` or start a harmless `claude --bg` task in a fresh session.

agent-view.notes: Background sessions consume normal quota and depend on the local supervisor staying alive.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.69+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe voice dictation as requiring v2.1.69 or later; tap mode requires v2.1.116 or later.

dependencies.label: Claude.ai account and microphone
dependencies.detail: Voice dictation requires Claude.ai authentication, local microphone access, and a local terminal or supported IDE surface.

limitations.label: Audio leaves the machine
limitations.detail: Official docs say recorded audio is streamed to Anthropic for transcription and is not processed locally.

limitations.label: Provider limitations
limitations.detail: Voice dictation is not available with direct API-key-only usage, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, remote web sessions, or SSH-only environments.

verification_steps.label: Voice command
verification_steps.detail: Start Claude Code locally and run `/voice`; the microphone check and prompt footer prove runtime availability.

voice-dictation.notes: Recorded audio is streamed for transcription and requires local microphone access.

dependencies.label: Voice dictation enabled
dependencies.detail: Tap mode only matters when voice dictation itself is enabled and available.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.116+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe tap mode as requiring Claude Code v2.1.116 or later.

voice-tap-mode.notes: Only changes voice dictation interaction style after voice is available.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.91+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe Ultraplan as a research preview requiring Claude Code v2.1.91 or later.

dependencies.label: Claude Code on the web and GitHub
dependencies.detail: Ultraplan runs on Claude Code on the web infrastructure and requires a web account plus a GitHub repository/cloud environment.

limitations.label: Provider limitations
limitations.detail: Ultraplan is not available on Bedrock, Vertex AI, or Microsoft Foundry routes.

limitations.label: Command only
limitations.detail: Invoke with `/ultraplan <prompt>` or the `ultraplan` keyword; LabKit does not launch the cloud planning session.

verification_steps.label: Command availability
verification_steps.detail: In a fresh Claude Code session, check `/help` or run `/ultraplan` with a harmless planning prompt.

ultraplan.notes: Cloud planning workflow; visible by default but command-only and account/provider gated.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.86+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe Ultrareview as a research preview requiring Claude Code v2.1.86 or later.

dependencies.label: Extra usage
dependencies.detail: After any included free runs, Ultrareview requires extra usage billing and normally costs more than local review.

limitations.label: Provider and policy limitations
limitations.detail: Ultrareview is not available on Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry, or organizations with Zero Data Retention enabled.

ultrareview.notes: Cloud review can incur extra usage and is unavailable on some provider or policy routes.

dependencies.label: GitHub and Claude Code on the web
dependencies.detail: The official command requires GitHub CLI access and Claude Code on the web access for the target pull request.

autofix-pr.notes: Cloud PR automation can push fixes; use only on repositories where that write path is acceptable.

dependencies.label: Agent View
dependencies.detail: Background sessions are managed by Agent View and the local per-user supervisor.

limitations.label: Local lifecycle
limitations.detail: Background sessions run locally, can stop on sleep/shutdown, and can leave worktrees that need cleanup.

background-session.notes: Background sessions can continue consuming quota and leave local worktrees to clean up.

side-question.notes: Command-only context-management helper; LabKit documents it but does not execute it.

context-visualizer.notes: Command-only inspection helper; safe to inspect manually in Claude Code.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.32+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe Agent Teams as requiring Claude Code v2.1.32 or later.

limitations.label: Experimental coordination
limitations.detail: Official docs call Agent Teams experimental and note limitations around session resumption, task coordination, and shutdown behavior.

agent-teams.notes: Experimental multi-agent coordination can increase quota use and file-conflict risk.

dependencies.label: Claude Code v2.1.117+
dependencies.detail: Official docs describe forked subagents as experimental and requiring Claude Code v2.1.117 or later.

limitations.label: Drops input isolation
limitations.detail: Forked subagents inherit the full conversation context, unlike normal subagents that start with a smaller isolated prompt.

fork-subagents.notes: Forked subagents inherit full conversation context, reducing isolation.

dependencies.label: Claude Code auto mode support
dependencies.detail: Use a current Claude Code build where `--permission-mode auto` and `permissions.defaultMode` are supported.

limitations.label: Research-preview safety model
limitations.detail: Auto mode approves low-risk tool calls through background safety checks; it is not OS sandboxing and it may be disabled by managed policy.

limitations.label: Old flag removed
limitations.detail: `--enable-auto-mode` was removed in favor of `--permission-mode auto`.

verification_steps.label: Permission mode
verification_steps.detail: Start Claude Code and inspect `/permissions` or `/status` to confirm the active permission mode.

auto-permissions.notes: Auto mode can approve low-risk tool calls without manual confirmation; it is not OS sandboxing.

dependencies.label: Claude.ai authentication
dependencies.detail: Official CLI docs describe `--channels` as requiring Claude.ai authentication.

dependencies.label: Channel-capable MCP/plugin source
dependencies.detail: A plugin or MCP server must advertise a Claude channel capability and be allowed by policy.

limitations.label: Research preview
limitations.detail: Channels are research-preview inbound notifications and may be restricted by managed allowlists.

channels.notes: Research-preview inbound notifications depend on account, plugin, and MCP allowlists.

limitations.label: Dangerous session flag
limitations.detail: This bypasses the approved channel allowlist for development and prompts for confirmation. LabKit documents it but will not launch it.

development-channels.notes: Development-only flag that bypasses approved channel allowlists; LabKit will not launch it.

limitations.label: Known removed
limitations.detail: `--enable-auto-mode` is documented as removed in v2.1.111. Use `--permission-mode auto` or `permissions.defaultMode` instead.

removed-auto-mode-flag.notes: Removed flag; use supported auto permission mode instead.

limitations.label: No public enable path
limitations.detail: Public leak analyses describe KAIROS as feature-flagged or roadmap work, but current official docs do not expose a user setting, env var, or command for enabling it.

kairos.notes: Leak-reported architecture with no supported public setting or command.

limitations.label: Part of unreleased KAIROS
limitations.detail: Official Auto Memory and Managed Agents dreaming exist, but leak-specific Claude Code Auto-Dream is not a supported public control and should not be confused with `autoMemoryEnabled`.

limitations.label: Managed Agents only
limitations.detail: Anthropic's public dreaming preview is for Claude Managed Agents on the platform, not a local Claude Code setting.

auto-dream.notes: Leak-reported KAIROS memory cycle; do not confuse it with public Auto Memory or managed-agent dreaming.

limitations.label: Unsupported leaked name
limitations.detail: Community reports conflict on whether a coordinator env var ever worked; current official docs point to Agent Teams via `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS` instead.

verification_steps.label: Use official replacement
verification_steps.detail: Enable `agent-teams` and verify with a fresh session instead of relying on leaked coordinator-mode names.

coordinator-mode.notes: Leak-reported orchestration mode; use official Agent Teams instead.

limitations.label: No public enable path
limitations.detail: Leak analyses describe Buddy as an experiment or teaser; current official docs do not expose it.

buddy.notes: Leak-reported companion experiment with no public enablement path.

limitations.label: Not in official command docs
limitations.detail: `/dream` is leak-reported and appears tied to unreleased KAIROS memory consolidation.

dream-command.notes: Leak-reported command tied to unreleased KAIROS behavior.

limitations.label: Use supported PR automation
limitations.detail: Leak-reported PR subscription tools are not public; use `/autofix-pr`, Code Review, channels, routines, or GitHub Actions instead.

pr-subscriptions.notes: Leak-reported PR tooling; use supported PR automation instead.

limitations.label: Use supported review commands
limitations.detail: Leak-reported `/bughunter` is not public; use `/review`, `/security-review`, `/simplify`, or `/ultrareview` instead.

bughunter.notes: Leak-reported review command; use supported review commands instead.

limitations.label: Internal and not user-facing
limitations.detail: Leak analyses discuss this as an internal/controversial behavior, not a supported user feature. LabKit will not enable it.

undercover-mode.notes: Internal/leak-reported mode involving hidden authorship signals; LabKit will not enable it.

limitations.label: Not user-facing
limitations.detail: Leak analyses describe this as a server-side or internal competitive-defense mechanism, not a local feature flag users can rely on.

anti-distillation.notes: Internal/server-side competitive-defense behavior, not a user-facing feature flag.

limitations.label: Known unlikely to work
limitations.detail: Public reports say `USER_TYPE=ant` is an internal employee path and likely requires server-side or build-time validation; setting it locally should not be treated as enabling hidden features.

internal-ant-user-type.notes: Internal employee path; local USER_TYPE changes should not be treated as unlocking features.

limitations.label: Unsafe internal testing
limitations.detail: Leak analyses associate this with disabling safety behavior for experiments. LabKit intentionally refuses to write it.

ablation-baseline.notes: Unsafe internal testing switch associated with safety ablation. LabKit refuses to write it.

limitations.label: Unsafe internal testing
limitations.detail: Leak analyses associate this with disabling command-injection protections. LabKit intentionally refuses to write it.

command-injection-check-bypass.notes: Unsafe internal testing switch associated with bypassing command-injection protections. LabKit refuses to write it.
